title: wedelia tehuantepecana
intro: Wedelia tehuantepecana, also known as Mexican plantain, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family native to Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. Some of the health benefits associated with Wedelia tehuantepecana include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
